Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 009 [Ša-zi-ga][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x [...]

(o 1') [...] ... [...]

o 2'2'

[...] TI TI [...]

(o 2') [...] ... [...]

o 3'3'

[...] AB₂ [...]

(o 3') [...] ... [...]

o 4'4'

[...] x [...]

(o 4') [...] ... [...]

o 5'5'

[...] IM KI.GAR 1niš ḪI.ḪI NU NITA u MUNUS x [...]

(o 5') You mix together [...] clay from a pit; [...] figurine of a man and a woman ...

o 6'6'

[...] ⸢ŠID⸣nu tu-nak-ka-ram-ma A [...]

(o 6') you recite [...]; you remove (it) and ... [...]

o 7'7'

[...] 15 ana pa-an BE MA sul-la iq-te-ru x [...]

(o 7') [...] on the right they ... in front of ... he has invited (him with) a prayer [...]

o 8'8'

[...] x IŠ ki-lal-le-e-šu₂-nu TIqe₂ [...]

(o 8') [...] ... you take both of them [...]

o 9'9'

[...] ⸢AG₂?⸣-šu-nu-ti NITA u MUNUS NI x [...]

(o 9') [...] ... to them; man and woman ... [...]

o 10'10'

[...] x kam-ma ša₂ lu₂AŠGAB IŠ i-x [...] ⸢TI⸣ ina I₃ AZ mut-tal-li-[ki ...]

(o 10') [...] ... the kammu-plant of the leatherworker ... [...] with the myrtle oil of the peddler [...]

o 11'11'

[...] A gišNU.UR₂.MA KU₇.KU₇ ana ŠA₃ ŠUB [...] ḪI.ḪI ina GUB₃-ka TIqe₂

(o 11') you pour a sweet pomegranate juice inside (it); you mix [...]; you take (it) in your left (hand).

o 12'12'

[...]-ka GIŠ₃-ka 3šu₂ TAG ŠU-ka [...] x ⸢ŠU⸣ MUNUS ša₂ GUB₃ ⸢TAG⸣ [...]

(o 12') You touch your [...] and your penis three times; ... your [...] hand; you grasp the woman's left hand; [...]

o 13'13'

[...] ⸢na⸣-a-ka ḫal-pa-ku gur-ša ina qi₂-⸢bit⸣ [pi]-i te-li-te d[...]1

(o 13') [...] lovemaking; I am clad in intercourse, at the command of the skilful goddess [...]".

o 14'14'

[... an]-⸢na⸣-a 3šu₂ ŠIDnu-ma NITA u MUNUS ŠA₃-šu₂-nu IL₂-šu-nu-ti ul i-nu-[uḫ-ḫu ...]

(o 14') You recite this [...] three times and man and woman will desire each other: they will not calm down. [...]

o 15'15'

[... NAM.BUR₂].⸢BI⸣ ḪUN.<GA₂> ḫa-a-a-at-ti ina A GAZIsar x [...]

(o 15') [...] namburbû-ritual for the calming of terror: ... [...] in mustard juice;

o 16'16'

[...] ⸢SAG?⸣-ka u₂-kal ZI TAG-ma 1niš uš-ta-aḫ-[ḫu ...]

(o 16') [...] he holds your head; he touches the throat1 and they have sexual intercourse together [...]

o 17'17'

[...]-šu₂ DAB-ma ana MUNUS-šu₂ u ana MUNUS BARti ŠA₃-šu₂ la₃ IL₂-šu₂ kit-pu-lu [...]

(o 17') [...] he seizes his [...] he has no desire for his wife or an unknown woman but they are entwined [...]

o 18'18'

[...] A x AN u₂IN.NU.UŠ ina NINDA šeIN.NU.ḪA GU₇-ma ŠA₃.ZI.[GA ...]

(o 18') [...] he eats ... maštakal-plant with an ennēnu-bread and the sexual desire [...]

o 19'19'

[...] ka-šip-ma mun-ga TUKUši bir-ka-šu₂ qa₂-an-na ⸢ka⸣-la-tu-šu₂ DU x [...]

(o 19') [...] he is bewitched and he is affected by cramps; his genitals are contracted; his testicles ... [...]

o 20'20'

[...] x-ma ana e-peš A₂.AŠ₂ <ŠA₃-šu₂> la₃ IL₂-šu₂ ni-iš [ŠA₃] DAB-ma ana MUNUS ⸢DU⸣ [...]

(o 20') [...] ... and he does not desire to fulfill (her) wish; he masters his potency and goes to a woman [...]

o 21'21'

[...] x-ma MUNUS IGI-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ GURra NA BI [...] x KI lu₂UŠ₂ [...]

(o 21') [...] ... and he sees the woman and his desire returns. That man [...] ... with a dead man [...]

o 22'22'

[...] x x ⸢KA?⸣ nik-ma ina KI.GAR dUTU.ŠU₂.⸢A?⸣ [...]

(o 22') [...] he is lying with ... and at the clay pit, (at) sunset [...].

o 23'23'

[... u₂]tar-muš u₂ḪAR.ḪAR u₂ak-tam u₂EME.UR.GI₇ DUB₂ [...]

(o 23') You crush [...] lupin, thyme, aktam-plant, hound's-tongue plant [...]

o 24'24'

[... ana] gišGEŠTIN SUR.RA šum-ma ana KAŠ ŠUB-ma EN₂ su-[zi ...]

(o 24') you pour [...] into pressed wine or beer and you recite for (your) desire the incantation: "Radiance [...] (may) the desire return!" and you keep drinking and [...]

o 25'25'

[... šag₄] ⸢zi⸣-ga gur? ana ŠA₃ ŠIDnu-ma NAG-MEŠ-ma [...]

o 26'26'

[...] ⸢u₂⸣EME.UR.GI₇ ina!(GIM) dUTU.ŠU₂.A tu-qad-daš ina še-ri₃ ana IGI dUTU x [...]

(o 26') You purify [...] hound's tongue at sunset; in the morning, in front of the sun [...] ...

o 27'27'

[...] x x ina GIR₂ ZABAR TA šur-ši-šu₂ ZIaḫ SUD₂ ina KAŠ ⸢NAG⸣ [...]

(o 27') You pull out [...] ... by its roots with a bronze sword; you pound (it); you make (him) drink (it) with beer; [...].

o 28'28'

[...] BURU₅.ḪABRUD.DAmušen NITA ina MUN NU₂al la₃ pa-tan i-al-lut x [...]

(o 28') You spread out [...] of a male rock-partridge in the salt; he swallows it on an empty stomach; ... [...] he drinks [...] ṣaṣuttu-plant and henbane ... with beer; [...]

o 29'29'

[... u₂ṣa]-ṣu-ut-tu₂ u₂ŠAKIR UŠ ina KAŠ ⸢NAG⸣ [...]

(rest of obverse missing)
(missing)

1The god's name is most probably Ištar, as the adjective teleʾû often qualifies the goddess. Yet we do not know how it was spelled here.

1 Both sentences have idiomatic meanings : rēša kullu , "to look after someone " and napišta lapātu , "to take an oath ", but it is not clear whether these pertain here.
